title: House Robber
description:
You are a professional robber planning to rob houses along a street. Each house has a certain amount of money stashed. The only constraint is that adjacent houses have security systems connected — if two adjacent houses are broken into on the same night, the police will be alerted.

Given an integer array `nums` representing the amount of money in each house, return the maximum amount of money you can rob without alerting the police.

**Recurrence:** At each house you either rob it (add its value + best from two houses back) or skip it (take best from previous house). `dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i])`.

**Space Optimization:** You only need the previous two values, so O(1) space is achievable.

**Constraints:**
- `1 <= nums.length <= 100`
- `0 <= nums[i] <= 400`
editorial: Use two variables prev2 and prev1 (representing dp[i-2] and dp[i-1]). For each num: curr = max(prev1, prev2+num); prev2=prev1; prev1=curr. Return prev1. O(n) time, O(1) space.

rexHints.level1: At each house, you have two choices: rob it or skip it. Think about which choice maximizes your total.
rexHints.level2: Define dp[i] as the max money robbing from houses 0..i. dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]). You skip or you rob the current house.
rexHints.level3: You only need the last two dp values. prev2=0, prev1=0. For each num: curr=max(prev1, prev2+num); prev2=prev1; prev1=curr. Return prev1.
